turns.content: My name is Alice and I am learning about RAG.
Nice to meet you, Alice! RAG, or Retrieval-Augmented Generation, is a technique that combines a retrieval step with a language model to produce grounded, factual responses.
What did I say my name was?
I work as a data scientist and I am evaluating RAG systems.
As a data scientist evaluating RAG systems, you will likely care about metrics like faithfulness, context precision, context recall, and answer relevancy.
Which metric should I prioritise for reducing hallucinations?
For reducing hallucinations, faithfulness is the most important metric — it directly measures whether every claim in the response is grounded in the retrieved context.
Given my role, which evaluation framework would you recommend?
I want to evaluate faithfulness. The threshold I need is 0.85.
Understood. You want faithfulness scores at or above 0.85. I will use this threshold to flag cases where the model introduces unsupported claims.
Good. Now, should I also measure context recall?

Yes, context recall complements faithfulness. Faithfulness checks whether the answer is grounded in context; context recall checks whether the retrieved context is complete enough to answer the question.
What threshold did I specify earlier?
Let us discuss three metrics: faithfulness, context precision, and context recall.
Sure. Faithfulness measures groundedness of the answer; context precision measures retrieval signal-to-noise ratio; context recall measures retrieval completeness.
Which one measures whether the retrieved chunks are relevant?
Context precision measures what fraction of the retrieved chunks are actually relevant to the question.
And which one checks completeness of retrieval?
Context recall checks whether all the information needed to answer the question is present in the retrieved chunks.
How many metrics did I mention at the start of our conversation?
I prefer concise responses — no longer than two sentences.
Understood. I will keep my responses to two sentences or fewer.
What is prompt injection?
My project is called 'SafeChat' and it is a customer support chatbot.
Got it — SafeChat is your customer support chatbot project. How can I help you evaluate it?
I need to check for PII leakage.
For PII leakage detection in SafeChat, you can use guardrails that scan both inputs and outputs for patterns like names, emails, phone numbers, and credit card numbers.
What is the name of my project?
Topic 1: RAG evaluation. Topic 2: Red teaming. I want to discuss them in order.
Understood. We will start with RAG evaluation and then move to red teaming.
Great. What are the core RAGAS metrics?
The core RAGAS metrics are faithfulness, answer relevancy, context precision, and context recall.
Now let us move to topic 2. What is DAN?
DAN (Do Anything Now) is a classic jailbreak prompt that attempts to make the model ignore its safety constraints by pretending to be an unrestricted alter-ego.
What was the first topic we agreed to discuss?
The system I am testing uses GPT-4o as the generator and Pinecone as the vector store.
Noted — your system uses GPT-4o as the generator and Pinecone as the vector store. That is a common production RAG setup.
What embedding model should I use with Pinecone?
For Pinecone, text-embedding-3-small from OpenAI is a good cost-effective choice; text-embedding-3-large gives better recall at higher cost.
What vector store did I say I am using?
I have a budget of $50 per month for API calls.
Understood — you have a $50 monthly API budget. I will factor that into my recommendations.
Which evaluation framework fits my budget?
With a $50 monthly budget, RAGAS with a Groq free-tier key is the most cost-effective option. For judge calls, Llama-3.1-8b on Groq is free and sufficient for most RAG evaluations.
What was my monthly budget?
I need tests for hallucination, PII leakage, and prompt injection — in that priority order.
Understood. Priority order: 1) hallucination, 2) PII leakage, 3) prompt injection.
Which should I implement first?
You should implement hallucination detection first, as it is your highest priority. The HallucinationMetric in DeepEval or the faithfulness metric in RAGAS are both good starting points.
And which is my second priority?
